annotations.label: Ring (informal)

anchor.snippet: a ring is an algebraic structure consisting of a set with two binary operations

annotations.note: The Mathlib `Ring` typeclass formalizes the standard ring axioms.

annotations.label: Ring (formal)

anchor.snippet: More formally, a ring is a set that is endowed with two binary operations

annotations.note: Mathlib's `Ring` class encodes the axiomatic definition.

annotations.label: Commutative ring

anchor.snippet: A commutative ring is a ring with a commutative multiplication

annotations.note: `CommRing` extends `Ring` with `CommMonoid`.

annotations.label: Ring axioms

anchor.snippet: A ring is a set R equipped with two binary operations

annotations.note: Same as the lead: `Ring` class assembles the axioms.

annotations.label: Rng

anchor.snippet: a structure with the same axiomatic definition but without the requirement for a multiplicative identity is instead called a

annotations.note: Mathlib's `NonUnitalRing` is the rng concept (associative ring without 1).

annotations.label: Division ring and field

anchor.snippet: A nonzero ring in which every nonzero element has a multiplicative inverse is called a division ring

annotations.note: `DivisionRing` and `Field` are defined in `Mathlib.Algebra.Field.Defs`.

annotations.label: Additive group of a ring is abelian

anchor.snippet: Although the definition requires that the additive group be abelian, this can be inferred from the other ring axioms

annotations.note: Mathlib bakes `AddCommGroup` into `Ring`; the proof that commutativity is redundant is not packaged as a named theorem.

annotations.label: Integers as a ring

anchor.snippet: The most familiar example of a ring is the set of all integers

annotations.note: ℤ has a `CommRing` instance via the integer constructions in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Basic properties of a ring

anchor.snippet: Some basic properties of a ring follow immediately from the axioms

annotations.note: Standard consequences (e.g. `mul_zero`, `neg_one_mul`, etc.) are in `Mathlib.Algebra.Ring.{Defs,Basic}`.

annotations.label: Integers modulo 4

anchor.snippet: The sum

annotations.note: `ZMod 4` gives the ring ℤ/4ℤ.

annotations.label: Ring of 2-by-2 matrices over a field

anchor.snippet: The set of 2-by-2 square matrices with entries in a field F

annotations.note: Special case of the general `Matrix n n R` ring instance.

annotations.label: Matrix ring over a ring

anchor.snippet: More generally, for any ring R , commutative or not, and any nonnegative integer n , the square n × n matrices with entries in R form a ring

annotations.note: The square matrix ring instance is in `Mathlib.Data.Matrix.Mul`.

annotations.label: Ring of integers (prototypical commutative ring)

anchor.snippet: The prototypical example is the ring of integers with the two operations of addition and multiplication

annotations.note: ℤ is a `CommRing` in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Rational, real, complex numbers as fields

anchor.snippet: The rational, real and complex numbers are commutative rings of a type called fields

annotations.note: ℚ, ℝ, ℂ all carry `Field` instances in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Unital associative algebra over commutative ring

anchor.snippet: A unital associative algebra over a commutative ring R is itself a ring as well as an R -module

annotations.note: The `Algebra R A` class encodes a unital associative R-algebra structure.

annotations.label: Polynomial algebra R[X]

anchor.snippet: The algebra R [ X ] of polynomials with coefficients in R

annotations.note: `Polynomial R` (notation `R[X]`) carries a `CommSemiring`/`CommRing` structure.

annotations.label: Continuous real-valued functions

anchor.snippet: The set of all continuous real-valued functions defined on the real line forms a commutative

annotations.note: `C(X, ℝ)` is a commutative ring via the general `ContinuousMap` ring instance.

annotations.label: Functions from a set to a ring

anchor.snippet: Let X be a set, and let R be a ring. Then the set of all functions from X to R forms a ring

annotations.note: Pointwise ring structure on `X → R` is provided by `Pi.ring` (and `Pi.commRing`).

annotations.label: Ring of quadratic integers

anchor.snippet: The ring of quadratic integers , the integral closure of

annotations.note: `Zsqrtd d` formalizes ℤ[√d], a subclass of quadratic integer rings, but not the full general construction.

annotations.label: Ring of profinite integers

anchor.snippet: The ring of profinite integers

annotations.note: No dedicated `ProfiniteIntegers` ring construction found in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Hecke ring

anchor.snippet: The Hecke ring , the ring generated by Hecke operators

annotations.note: No formalization of the Hecke ring of Hecke operators found in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Boolean ring on a power set

anchor.snippet: If S is a set, then the power set of S becomes a ring if we define addition to be the symmetric difference of sets and multiplication to be intersection

annotations.note: `BooleanRing` class exists; the specific instance on `Set S` via symmetric difference/intersection is not packaged as a `BooleanRing` instance in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Matrix ring (noncommutative)

anchor.snippet: For any ring R and any natural number n , the set of all square n -by- n matrices with entries from R , forms a ring with matrix addition and matrix multiplication as operations

annotations.note: `Matrix n n R` is a ring (noncommutative in general).

annotations.label: Endomorphism ring of an abelian group

anchor.snippet: If G is an abelian group , then the endomorphisms of G form a ring, the endomorphism ring

annotations.note: `AddMonoid.End G` carries a ring instance giving the endomorphism ring of an abelian group.

annotations.label: Endomorphism ring of an elliptic curve

anchor.snippet: The endomorphism ring of an elliptic curve

annotations.note: No endomorphism ring construction for elliptic curves found in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Group ring

anchor.snippet: If G is a group and R is a ring, the group ring of G over R is a free module over R having G as basis

annotations.note: `MonoidAlgebra R G` is the group/monoid ring construction.

annotations.label: Ring of differential operators

anchor.snippet: The ring of differential operators (depending on the context)

annotations.note: No general ring of differential operators (Weyl algebra / D-modules) construction found in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Natural numbers are not a ring

anchor.snippet: The set of natural numbers

annotations.note: Mathlib has `Nat` as `CommSemiring` but no theorem stating ℕ is not a ring.

annotations.label: Convolution rng (Dirac delta)

anchor.snippet: Let R be the set of all continuous functions on the real line that vanish outside a bounded interval that depends on the function

annotations.note: The convolution rng of compactly supported continuous functions with Dirac identity is not in Mathlib.

anchors.snippet: Let R be the set of all continuous functions on the real line that vanish outside a bounded interval that depends on the function

annotations.label: Product of a sequence in a ring

anchor.snippet: given a sequence

annotations.note: `Finset.prod` (`∏`) formalizes finite products in a commutative monoid/ring.

annotations.label: Nonnegative integer powers

anchor.snippet: As a special case, one can define nonnegative integer powers of an element a of a ring

annotations.note: `Monoid.npow`/`HPow` provide ℕ-indexed powers in a monoid/ring.

annotations.label: Left zero divisor

anchor.snippet: A left zero divisor of a ring R is an element a in the ring such that there exists a nonzero element b of R such that

annotations.note: Mathlib uses the dual `IsLeftRegular`/`NoZeroDivisors`; there is no standalone `IsLeftZeroDivisor` predicate.

annotations.label: Nilpotent element

anchor.snippet: A nilpotent element is an element a such that

annotations.note: `IsNilpotent x` is `∃ n, x ^ n = 0`.

annotations.label: Nilpotent is a zero divisor

anchor.snippet: A nilpotent element in a nonzero ring is necessarily a zero divisor

annotations.note: Nilpotents-as-non-regular results exist in `Mathlib.RingTheory.Nilpotent.Lemmas` but no direct named lemma 'nilpotent ⇒ zero divisor' was located.

annotations.label: Idempotent element

anchor.snippet: is an element such that

annotations.note: `IsIdempotentElem a := a * a = a`.

annotations.label: Unit in a ring

anchor.snippet: A unit is an element a having a multiplicative inverse

annotations.note: `IsUnit` predicate exists; `Units R` (`Rˣ`) bundles the group of units.

annotations.label: Subring

anchor.snippet: A subset S of R is called a subring if any one of the following equivalent conditions holds

annotations.note: `Subring R` is a structure bundling submonoid+subgroup conditions.

annotations.label: Intersection of subrings is a subring

anchor.snippet: An intersection of subrings is a subring

annotations.note: `Subring` carries a `CompleteLattice` instance whose `inf`/`iInf` is intersection.

annotations.label: Subring generated by a set

anchor.snippet: the smallest subring of R containing E is the intersection of all subrings of R containing E

annotations.note: `Subring.closure s` is the smallest subring containing `s`.

annotations.label: Characteristic subring and characteristic

anchor.snippet: the smallest subring of R is called the characteristic subring of R

annotations.note: `CharP R p` and `ringChar` capture the characteristic; the prime/characteristic subring itself is not separately named.

annotations.label: Center and centralizer of a ring

anchor.snippet: Then Z( R ) is a subring of R , called the center of R

annotations.note: `Subring.center` and `Subring.centralizer` are defined in `Mathlib.Algebra.Ring.Subring.Basic`.

annotations.label: Left ideal

anchor.snippet: A left ideal of R is a nonempty subset I of R such that for any

annotations.note: Left ideals are `Submodule R R`; Mathlib's main `Ideal R` (defined the same way) is geared toward commutative use.

annotations.label: Right ideal and two-sided ideal

anchor.snippet: Similarly, a right ideal is a subset I such that

annotations.note: `TwoSidedIdeal R` exists; right ideals via `Submodule Rᵐᵒᵖ R`.

annotations.label: Principal ideal

anchor.snippet: If x is in R , then Rx is a left ideal, and xR is a right ideal; they are called the principal left ideal and right ideal generated by x

annotations.note: `Ideal.span {x}` gives the principal ideal generated by `x`.

annotations.label: Simple ring

anchor.snippet: A ring is simple if it is nonzero and it has no proper nonzero two-sided ideals

annotations.note: `IsSimpleRing` is defined in `Mathlib.RingTheory.SimpleRing.Defs`.

annotations.label: Commutative simple rings are fields

anchor.snippet: A commutative simple ring is precisely a field

annotations.note: Mathlib has the equivalence between commutative simple rings and fields in `SimpleRing.Basic`.

annotations.label: Noetherian and Artinian rings

anchor.snippet: a ring in which there is no strictly increasing infinite chain of left ideals is called a left Noetherian ring

annotations.note: `IsNoetherianRing` and `IsArtinianRing` are defined in `Mathlib.RingTheory.{Noetherian,Artinian}.Defs`.

annotations.label: Hopkins–Levitzki theorem

anchor.snippet: It is a somewhat surprising fact that a left Artinian ring is left Noetherian

annotations.note: The Hopkins–Levitzki theorem is in `Mathlib.RingTheory.HopkinsLevitzki`.

annotations.label: Prime ideal

anchor.snippet: A proper ideal P of R is called a prime ideal if for any elements

annotations.note: `Ideal.IsPrime` is the standard prime-ideal predicate.

annotations.label: Ring homomorphism

anchor.snippet: A homomorphism from a ring

annotations.note: `RingHom α β` (`α →+* β`) bundles ring homomorphisms.

annotations.label: Ring isomorphism

anchor.snippet: A ring homomorphism f is said to be an isomorphism if there exists an inverse homomorphism to f

annotations.note: `RingEquiv R S` (`R ≃+* S`) is the ring isomorphism bundle.

annotations.label: Reduction modulo 4 homomorphism

anchor.snippet: The function that maps each integer x to its remainder modulo 4

annotations.note: `Int.castRingHom (ZMod 4)` is the reduction mod 4 ring homomorphism.

annotations.label: Inner automorphism

anchor.snippet: If u is a unit element in a ring R , then

annotations.note: Inner automorphisms exist for groups via `MulAut.conj`; a dedicated ring-inner-automorphism bundle was not located.

annotations.label: Frobenius homomorphism

anchor.snippet: Let R be a commutative ring of prime characteristic p

annotations.note: `frobenius R p : R →+* R` raises to the p-th power.

annotations.label: Galois group

anchor.snippet: The Galois group of a field extension L / K is the set of all automorphisms of L whose restrictions to K are the identity

annotations.note: `L ≃ₐ[K] L` is the Galois group (with `IsGalois` capturing the Galois-extension property).

annotations.label: Representation of an algebra

anchor.snippet: An algebra homomorphism from a k -algebra to the endomorphism algebra of a vector space over k is called a representation of the algebra

annotations.note: `Representation k G V` is defined as `G →* (V →ₗ[k] V)ˣ`; algebra-representation analogue available via `AlgHom`.

annotations.label: Kernel of a ring homomorphism

anchor.snippet: Given a ring homomorphism f : R → S , the set of all elements mapped to 0 by f is called the kernel of f

annotations.note: `RingHom.ker f` is the ideal kernel of a ring hom.

annotations.label: Homomorphism to center = algebra structure

anchor.snippet: To give a ring homomorphism from a commutative ring R to a ring A with image contained in the center of A is the same as to give a structure of an algebra over R to A

annotations.note: `RingHom.toAlgebra` constructs an algebra structure from a hom into the center, but the bijective correspondence is not packaged as a single equivalence.

annotations.label: Quotient ring

anchor.snippet: the quotient ring R / I is the set of cosets of I together with the operations

annotations.note: `R ⧸ I` is the quotient ring with `Ideal.Quotient.mk` as the projection.

annotations.label: Universal property of quotient ring

anchor.snippet: there is a canonical homomorphism

annotations.note: `Ideal.Quotient.lift` formalizes the universal property of the quotient.

annotations.label: First isomorphism theorem for rings

anchor.snippet: For any ring homomorphism f : R → S , invoking the universal property with

annotations.note: The first iso theorem for rings is `RingHom.quotientKerEquivOfSurjective`.

annotations.label: Module over a ring

anchor.snippet: given a ring R , an R -module M is an abelian group equipped with an operation

annotations.note: `Module R M` (over a semiring R, abelian monoid M).

annotations.label: Ring homomorphism induces module structure

anchor.snippet: Any ring homomorphism induces a structure of a module

annotations.note: `Module.compHom` pulls back a module along a ring hom (restriction of scalars).

annotations.label: Direct product of rings

anchor.snippet: is called the direct product of R with S

annotations.note: `Prod` and `Pi` ring instances formalize direct products of rings.

annotations.label: Chinese remainder theorem

anchor.snippet: Then the Chinese remainder theorem says there is a canonical ring isomorphism

annotations.note: `Ideal.quotientInfRingEquivPiQuotient` is the CRT for ideals.

anchors.snippet: Then the Chinese remainder theorem says there is a canonical ring isomorphism

annotations.label: Finite direct product as direct sum of ideals

anchor.snippet: A "finite" direct product may also be viewed as a direct sum of ideals

annotations.note: Decomposition via complete orthogonal idempotents is formalized; the precise direct-sum-of-ideals identification is not a single named lemma.

anchors.snippet: A "finite" direct product may also be viewed as a direct sum of ideals

annotations.label: Polynomial ring

anchor.snippet: It is called the polynomial ring over R

annotations.note: `Polynomial R` is the univariate polynomial ring; `MvPolynomial σ R` for multivariate.

annotations.label: Properties of polynomial rings

anchor.snippet: If R is an integral domain , then R [ t ] is also an integral domain

annotations.note: `Polynomial R` inherits `IsDomain` from `R`.

annotations.label: Substitution homomorphism

anchor.snippet: Given an element x of S , one can consider the ring homomorphism

annotations.note: `Polynomial.aeval x : R[X] →ₐ S` is evaluation/substitution at `x`.

annotations.label: Subalgebra generated by t^2 and t^3

anchor.snippet: In other words, it is the subalgebra of k [ t ] generated by

annotations.note: No specific formalization of the `k[t²,t³]` example.

annotations.label: Derivative from polynomial substitution

anchor.snippet: let f be a polynomial in one variable, that is, an element in a polynomial ring R

annotations.note: `Polynomial.derivative` is the formal derivative; the substitution-of-`t+h` derivation is not formalized as such.

annotations.label: Universal property of polynomial ring

anchor.snippet: The substitution is a special case of the universal property of a polynomial ring

annotations.note: `MvPolynomial.aeval_unique` (and the univariate analog) encodes the universal property.

annotations.label: Monic polynomial factors over an extension

anchor.snippet: Given a non-constant monic polynomial f in R [ t ] , there exists a ring S containing R such that f is a product of linear factors

annotations.note: `SplittingField` realizes this over fields; the statement for general commutative rings is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Hilbert's Nullstellensatz

anchor.snippet: The Hilbert's Nullstellensatz (theorem of zeros) states that there is a natural one-to-one correspondence

annotations.note: Hilbert's Nullstellensatz is proved in `Mathlib.RingTheory.Nullstellensatz`.

annotations.label: Formal power series ring

anchor.snippet: A formal power series ring

annotations.note: `PowerSeries R` is the formal power series ring (and `MvPowerSeries` for multivariate).

annotations.label: Matrix ring M_n(R)

anchor.snippet: The set of all square matrices of size n with entries in R forms a ring with the entry-wise addition and the usual matrix multiplication. It is called the matrix ring

annotations.note: `Matrix n n R` carries the matrix ring instance.

annotations.label: Endomorphism ring End_R(U)

anchor.snippet: the set of all R -linear maps from U to itself forms a ring with addition that is of function and multiplication that is of composition of functions ; it is called the endomorphism ring

annotations.note: `Module.End R M` is `M →ₗ[R] M` with ring structure.

annotations.label: Matrix ring as endomorphism ring

anchor.snippet: As in linear algebra, a matrix ring may be canonically interpreted as an endomorphism ring

annotations.note: `Matrix.toLinAlgEquiv` identifies square matrices with the endomorphism algebra of a finite free module.

annotations.label: Schur's lemma

anchor.snippet: Schur's lemma says that if U is a simple right R -module

annotations.note: Schur's lemma (endomorphism ring of a simple module is a division ring) is in `SimpleModule.Basic`; categorical version in `CategoryTheory.Preadditive.Schur`.

annotations.label: Artin–Wedderburn theorem

anchor.snippet: The Artin–Wedderburn theorem states any semisimple ring (cf. below) is of this form

annotations.note: Artin–Wedderburn is `IsSemisimpleRing.exists_algEquiv_pi_matrix_divisionRing` in `SimpleModule.WedderburnArtin`.

annotations.label: Morita equivalence of R and M_n(R)

anchor.snippet: are Morita equivalent : the category of right modules of R is equivalent to the category of right modules

annotations.note: Morita equivalence between `R` and `Mₙ(R)` (category-of-modules equivalence) is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Filtered colimit (union) of rings

anchor.snippet: Then the union (or filtered colimit ) of R i

annotations.note: `Ring.DirectLimit` realizes directed/filtered colimits of rings.

annotations.label: Every ring is a filtered colimit of finitely generated subrings

anchor.snippet: Any ring is the filtered colimit (union) of its finitely generated subrings

annotations.note: No theorem stating every ring is the filtered colimit of its f.g. subrings was located.

annotations.label: Projective limit of rings

anchor.snippet: A projective limit (or a filtered limit ) of rings is defined as follows

annotations.note: Categorical limits in `CommRingCat`/`RingCat` exist; no separately bundled 'projective limit of rings' constructor.

annotations.label: Localization of a ring

anchor.snippet: is called the localization of R with respect to S

annotations.note: `Localization S` and `IsLocalization` axiomatize ring localization.

annotations.label: Localization of a module

anchor.snippet: If M is a left R -module, then the localization of M with respect to S is given by a change of rings

annotations.note: `LocalizedModule` and `IsLocalizedModule` formalize module localization.

annotations.label: Prime ideals under localization

anchor.snippet: is a bijection between the set of all prime ideals in R disjoint from S

annotations.note: `IsLocalization.orderIsoOfPrime` gives the bijection between primes of the localization and primes disjoint from S.

annotations.label: Localization is exact

anchor.snippet: The localization is exact

annotations.note: Exactness of localization is proven for `LocalizedModule`/`IsLocalizedModule`.

annotations.label: Exactness from local exactness

anchor.snippet: Conversely, if

annotations.note: The 'local-to-global' exactness criterion is in Mathlib via localization at maximal ideals.

annotations.label: Completion of a ring at an ideal

anchor.snippet: The completion of R at I is the projective limit

annotations.note: `AdicCompletion I R` is the I-adic completion.

annotations.label: Krull's intersection theorem

anchor.snippet: The latter homomorphism is injective if R is a Noetherian integral domain and I is a proper ideal

annotations.note: Krull's intersection theorem is proven in `Mathlib.RingTheory.Filtration` for Noetherian local rings and domains.

annotations.label: Ring of p-adic integers

anchor.snippet: it is called the ring of p -adic integers

annotations.note: `PadicInt p` is the ring of p-adic integers ℤ_p.

annotations.label: p-adic absolute value

anchor.snippet: The p -adic absolute value on

annotations.note: `padicNorm p q` is the p-adic absolute value on ℚ.

annotations.label: Cohen structure theorem

anchor.snippet: This owns to the Cohen structure theorem , which says, roughly, that a complete local ring tends to look like a formal power series ring or a quotient of it

annotations.note: Cohen's structure theorem is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Free ring

anchor.snippet: Let F be a free ring (that is, free algebra over the integers) with the set X of symbols

annotations.note: `FreeRing α` is the free (associative, unital) ring on a set.

annotations.label: Universal property of free ring

anchor.snippet: A free ring satisfies the universal property: any function from the set X to a ring R factors through F

annotations.note: `FreeRing.lift` provides the universal map.

annotations.label: Ring with generators and relations

anchor.snippet: the quotient ring of F by the ideal generated by E is called the ring with generators X and relations E

annotations.note: `RingQuot` and quotients by `Ideal.span` realize 'generators and relations' but no single named bundle 'GeneratorsAndRelations'.

annotations.label: Tensor product of algebras

anchor.snippet: Let A , B be algebras over a commutative ring R . Then the tensor product of R -modules

annotations.note: `Algebra.TensorProduct R A B` gives the algebra tensor product.

annotations.label: Domain and integral domain

anchor.snippet: A nonzero ring with no nonzero zero-divisors is called a domain . A commutative domain is called an integral domain

annotations.note: `IsDomain` (with `[CommRing]` for integral domains) is in `Mathlib.Algebra.Ring.Defs`.

annotations.label: Principal ideal domain

anchor.snippet: A principal ideal domain is an integral domain in which every ideal is principal

annotations.note: `IsPrincipalIdealRing` plus `IsDomain` gives PID.

annotations.label: Unique factorization domain

anchor.snippet: a unique factorization domain (UFD), an integral domain in which every nonunit element is a product of prime elements

annotations.note: `UniqueFactorizationMonoid` on a `CommMonoidWithZero` formalizes UFDs.

annotations.label: Structure theorem for finitely generated modules over a PID

anchor.snippet: the most important one is the structure theorem for finitely generated modules over a principal ideal domain

annotations.note: `Module.equiv_free_prod_directSum` is the PID structure theorem in `Mathlib.Algebra.Module.PID`.

anchors.snippet: the most important one is the structure theorem for finitely generated modules over a principal ideal domain

annotations.label: Regular local rings are UFDs

anchor.snippet: A regular local ring is a UFD

annotations.note: Auslander–Buchsbaum (regular local ⇒ UFD) is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Division ring

anchor.snippet: A division ring is a ring such that every non-zero element is a unit

annotations.note: `DivisionRing` class in `Mathlib.Algebra.Field.Defs`.

annotations.label: Centralizer in a division ring

anchor.snippet: Any centralizer in a division ring is also a division ring

annotations.note: No named theorem that the centralizer of any subset in a division ring is itself a division ring.

annotations.label: Wedderburn's little theorem

anchor.snippet: every finite domain (in particular finite division ring) is a field

annotations.note: Wedderburn's little theorem is in `Mathlib.RingTheory.LittleWedderburn`.

annotations.label: Modules over a division ring are free

anchor.snippet: Every module over a division ring is a free module (has a basis)

annotations.note: Vector spaces over a division ring are free (every module admits a basis).

annotations.label: Cyclic algebra

anchor.snippet: A cyclic algebra , introduced by L. E. Dickson , is a generalization of a quaternion algebra

annotations.note: Cyclic algebras are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Semisimple module and ring

anchor.snippet: A semisimple module is a direct sum of simple modules. A semisimple ring is a ring that is semisimple as a left module

annotations.note: `IsSemisimpleModule` and abbrev `IsSemisimpleRing := IsSemisimpleModule R R`.

annotations.label: Division ring is semisimple

anchor.snippet: A division ring is semisimple (and simple )

annotations.note: Instance making a division ring semisimple is present in `SimpleModule.Basic`.

annotations.label: Matrix ring over a division ring is semisimple

anchor.snippet: For any division ring D and positive integer n , the matrix ring

annotations.note: Matrix rings over a division ring are shown semisimple in `WedderburnArtin`.

annotations.label: Maschke's theorem

anchor.snippet: For a field k and finite group G , the group ring kG is semisimple if and only if the characteristic of k does not divide the order of G

annotations.note: Maschke's theorem is formalized in `Mathlib.RepresentationTheory.Maschke`.

annotations.label: Clifford algebras are semisimple

anchor.snippet: Clifford algebras are semisimple

annotations.note: Mathlib defines `CliffordAlgebra` but no semisimplicity instance is provided.

annotations.label: Weyl algebra is simple but not semisimple

anchor.snippet: The Weyl algebra over a field is a simple ring , but it is not semisimple

annotations.note: The Weyl algebra is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Modules over a semisimple ring are semisimple

anchor.snippet: Any module over a semisimple ring is semisimple

annotations.note: `IsSemisimpleRing.isSemisimpleModule` makes every module over a semisimple ring semisimple.

annotations.label: Characterizations of semisimple rings

anchor.snippet: For a ring R , the following are equivalent

annotations.note: Various equivalences for `IsSemisimpleRing` exist (and the Artin–Wedderburn equivalence) but a single tfae lemma matching the Wikipedia list isn't present.

annotations.label: Separable algebra

anchor.snippet: A unital associative algebra A over a field k is said to be separable if the base extension

annotations.note: Mathlib has `Algebra.IsSeparable` for field extensions; the more general `separable algebra` (every base change is semisimple) is not packaged directly.

annotations.label: Central simple algebra

anchor.section: Central simple algebra and Brauer group
anchor.snippet: a k -algebra is central if its center is k and is simple if it is a simple ring

annotations.note: `Algebra.IsCentralSimple` formalizes central simple algebras.

annotations.label: Skolem–Noether theorem

anchor.section: Central simple algebra and Brauer group
anchor.snippet: The Skolem–Noether theorem states any automorphism of a central simple algebra is inner

annotations.note: Skolem–Noether is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Similarity of central simple algebras

anchor.section: Central simple algebra and Brauer group
anchor.snippet: Two central simple algebras A and B are said to be similar if there are integers n and m

annotations.note: Brauer similarity is the setoid `Brauer.CSA_Setoid` in `BrauerGroup.Defs`.

annotations.label: Brauer group

anchor.section: Central simple algebra and Brauer group
anchor.snippet: form an abelian group called the Brauer group of k

annotations.note: `BrauerGroup K := Quotient (Brauer.CSA_Setoid K)`.

annotations.label: Brauer groups of standard fields

anchor.section: Central simple algebra and Brauer group
anchor.snippet: Br( k ) is trivial if k is a finite field or an algebraically closed field

annotations.note: Vanishing of `Br(k)` for finite or algebraically closed `k` is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Br(F/k) for finite Galois extensions

anchor.section: Central simple algebra and Brauer group
anchor.snippet: If the extension is finite and Galois, then Br( F / k ) is canonically isomorphic to

annotations.note: The identification of relative Brauer groups with `H²(Gal, F^×)` is not formalized.

annotations.label: Azumaya algebra

anchor.section: Central simple algebra and Brauer group
anchor.snippet: Azumaya algebras generalize the notion of central simple algebras to a commutative local ring

annotations.note: Azumaya algebras are formalized in `Mathlib.Algebra.Azumaya.Defs`.

annotations.label: Valuation

anchor.snippet: a valuation v is a group homomorphism from the multiplicative group

annotations.note: `Valuation R Γ₀` is a multiplicative-norm-style valuation on a ring.

annotations.label: Valuation ring

anchor.snippet: The valuation ring of v is the subring of K consisting of zero and all nonzero f such that

annotations.note: `ValuationSubring K` and the `ValuationRing` typeclass capture valuation rings.

annotations.label: Valuation on formal Laurent series

anchor.snippet: The field of formal Laurent series

annotations.note: Mathlib has `LaurentSeries`/`HahnSeries` and order valuations on these, but the example's specific valuation packaging isn't named.

annotations.label: Group ring with totally ordered group

anchor.snippet: given a field k and a totally ordered abelian group G

annotations.note: `HahnSeries` generalizes `k[[G]]` and carries a valuation; not packaged as the exact group-ring example.

anchors.snippet: given a field k and a totally ordered abelian group G

annotations.label: Associative algebra

anchor.snippet: An associative algebra is a ring that is also a vector space over a field such that the scalar multiplication is compatible with the ring multiplication

annotations.note: `Algebra R A` over a commutative (semi)ring `R` formalizes associative algebras.

annotations.label: Topological ring

anchor.snippet: A ring R is a topological ring if its set of elements R is given a topology

annotations.note: `IsTopologicalRing R` requires continuity of +, -, ×.

annotations.label: Lambda-ring

anchor.snippet: A λ-ring is a commutative ring R together with operations

annotations.note: λ-rings are not formalized in Mathlib (binomial rings are, but not λ-rings).

annotations.label: Totally ordered ring

anchor.snippet: A totally ordered ring is a ring with a total ordering that is compatible with ring operations

annotations.note: `IsOrderedRing`/`IsStrictOrderedRing` (with a `LinearOrder`) formalize totally ordered rings.

annotations.label: Integral cohomology ring

anchor.section: Cohomology ring of a topological space
anchor.snippet: To any topological space X one can associate its integral cohomology ring

annotations.note: Singular cohomology with its cup product ring structure is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Burnside ring of a group

anchor.snippet: To any group is associated its Burnside ring which uses a ring to describe the various ways the group can act on a finite set

annotations.note: The Burnside ring is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Representation ring

anchor.section: Representation ring of a group ring
anchor.snippet: To any group ring or Hopf algebra is associated its representation ring

annotations.note: The representation ring of a group is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Function field of an algebraic variety

anchor.section: Function field of an irreducible algebraic variety
anchor.snippet: To any irreducible algebraic variety is associated its function field

annotations.note: `Scheme.functionField` defines the function field of an integral scheme.

annotations.label: Face ring (Stanley–Reisner ring)

anchor.section: Face ring of a simplicial complex
anchor.snippet: Every simplicial complex has an associated face ring, also called its Stanley–Reisner ring

annotations.note: Stanley–Reisner rings are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Ring as monoid in Ab

anchor.snippet: Every ring can be thought of as a monoid in Ab , the category of abelian groups

annotations.note: Monoid objects in monoidal categories are formalized; the specific equivalence `Ring ≃ Mon_(Ab)` is not a single named theorem.

annotations.label: Ring as endomorphism ring of an abelian X-group

anchor.snippet: any ring can be viewed as the endomorphism ring of some abelian X -group

annotations.note: This category-theoretic characterization is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Ring as preadditive category with one object

anchor.snippet: Any ring can be seen as a preadditive category with a single object

annotations.note: Preadditive categories are formalized; the explicit `Ring ↔ one-object preadditive category` bridge is not packaged.

annotations.label: Rng (generalization)

anchor.snippet: A rng is the same as a ring, except that the existence of a multiplicative identity is not assumed

annotations.note: `NonUnitalRing` is Mathlib's rng class.

annotations.label: Nonassociative ring

anchor.snippet: A nonassociative ring is an algebraic structure that satisfies all of the ring axioms except the associative property and the existence of a multiplicative identity

annotations.note: `NonUnitalNonAssocRing` drops associativity and unit from `Ring`.

annotations.label: Semiring

anchor.snippet: A semiring (sometimes rig ) is obtained by weakening the assumption that

annotations.note: `Semiring` (additive structure only a commutative monoid).

annotations.label: Non-negative integers as a semiring

anchor.snippet: the non-negative integers

annotations.note: `ℕ` is a `CommSemiring`.

annotations.label: Ring object in a category

anchor.snippet: A ring object in C is an object R equipped with morphisms

annotations.note: Monoid objects in monoidal categories exist; an explicit `RingObject`/`Ring_` in a general category isn't packaged.

annotations.label: Ring scheme

anchor.snippet: a ring scheme over a base scheme S is a ring object in the category of S -schemes

annotations.note: Ring schemes are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Witt vector ring scheme W_n

anchor.snippet: One example is the ring scheme W n over

annotations.note: `WittVector` exists as a ring construction but not as a ring scheme.

annotations.label: Ring spectrum

anchor.snippet: a ring spectrum is a spectrum X together with a multiplication

annotations.note: Ring spectra (stable homotopy theory) are not formalized in Mathlib.
